response:
Sec. 1  
[TO DETERMINE WHEN PUBLISHER A'S OFFER IS MORE LUCRATIVE THAN PUBLISHER B'S OFFER, WE COMPARE THE TOTAL EARNINGS FROM EACH PUBLISHER.]

Sec. 2  
[THE TOTAL EARNINGS FROM PUBLISHER A CAN BE REPRESENTED AS 3000 PLUS 5% OF THE SALES REVENUE, WHICH IS 3000 + 0.05X.]

Sec. 3  
[THE TOTAL EARNINGS FROM PUBLISHER B CAN BE REPRESENTED AS 1500 PLUS 10% OF THE SALES REVENUE, WHICH IS 1500 + 0.10X.]

Sec. 4  
[TO FIND WHEN PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE, WE SET UP THE INEQUALITY 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X.]

Sec. 5  
[SOLVING THE INEQUALITY INEQUALITY 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X INVOLVES SUBTRACTING 1500 FROM BOTH SIDES, RESULTING IN 1500 + 0.05X > 0.10X.]

Sec. 6  
[SUBTRACTING 0.05X FROM BOTH SIDES OF THE INEQUALITY GIVES 1500 > 0.05X, WHICH SIMPLIFIES TO X < 30000.]

Sec. 7  
[THE INEQUALITY X < 30000 INDICATES THAT PUBLISHER A'S OFFER IS MORE LUCRATIVE WHEN THE TOTAL SALES REVENUE IS LESS THAN $30,000.]

Sec. 8  
[WITHIN THE ANTICIPATED SALES REVENUE RANGE OF $10,000 TO $40,000, PUBLISHER A'S OFFER IS MORE BENEFICIAL WHEN THE SALES REVENUE IS BETWEEN $10,000 AND $30,000.]
